title: festuca rubra subsp. litoralis
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. litoralis, also known as coastal red fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the genus Festuca and is commonly found in coastal areas, particularly in Europe and North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. litoralis include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Festuca rubra subsp. litoralis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
